report:
## Profitability

Across the three companies, Coca-Cola (KO) stands out for its exceptional profit margins relative to revenue, while PepsiCo (PEP) generates far larger absolute revenues but has seen profitability soften recently, and Costco (COST) operates on thin margins despite massive and rapidly growing top-line revenues. KO's FY2025 revenues of ~$47.9B [KO:Q3] supported net income of ~$13.1B [KO:Q7] and operating income of ~$13.8B [KO:Q11], implying net and operating margins in the high-20s to nearly 30% range — the strongest margin profile of the three. PEP's FY2025 revenues reached ~$93.9B [PEP:Q3], roughly double KO's, yet net income declined to ~$8.2B [PEP:Q7] from a peak of ~$9.6B in FY2024 [PEP:Q6], and operating income slipped to ~$11.5B [PEP:Q11] from ~$12.9B in FY2024 [PEP:Q10], signaling margin compression. COST's revenues surged to ~$275B in FY2025 [COST:Q3], but its net income of ~$8.1B [COST:Q7] and operating income of ~$10.4B [COST:Q11] reflect the low-margin, high-volume warehouse retail model — net margins of roughly 3%. That said, COST's absolute profit trajectory is the most consistently upward of the three, with net income growing from ~$5.8B in FY2022 [COST:Q4] to ~$8.1B in FY2025 [COST:Q7]. KO's gross profit margin is also the highest, with gross profit of ~$29.5B on ~$47.9B in revenues [KO:Q15, KO:Q3], versus PEP's ~$50.9B gross profit on ~$93.9B revenues [PEP:Q15, PEP:Q3] — both well above COST's structurally thin gross margins.

**KO:** Coca-Cola delivers the highest margin profile among the three, with FY2025 net income of ~$13.1B [KO:Q7] on revenues of ~$47.9B [KO:Q3], implying a net margin approaching 27%. Operating income rebounded strongly to ~$13.8B in FY2025 [KO:Q11] after a dip to ~$10.0B in FY2024 [KO:Q10], and gross profit has grown steadily each year, reaching ~$29.5B in FY2025 [KO:Q15], underscoring the durable pricing power of its brand-heavy beverage portfolio.

**PEP:** PepsiCo generates the largest absolute revenues of the three — ~$93.9B in FY2025 [PEP:Q3] — but its profitability has come under pressure, with net income declining to ~$8.2B [PEP:Q7] from a recent peak of ~$9.6B in FY2024 [PEP:Q6], and operating income falling back to ~$11.5B [PEP:Q11] from ~$12.9B [PEP:Q10], suggesting rising costs or competitive headwinds are squeezing margins despite continued top-line scale.

**COST:** Costco's profitability story is one of consistent absolute dollar growth within a structurally low-margin model: net income has risen steadily from ~$5.8B in FY2022 [COST:Q4] to ~$8.1B in FY2025 [COST:Q7], and operating income reached ~$10.4B in FY2025 [COST:Q11], even as revenues of ~$275B [COST:Q3] keep net margins around 3%. The trajectory is the most uninterrupted upward of the three companies, reflecting strong membership-driven volume growth.

---

quality.notes: Exceptionally well-structured report with clear three-way comparisons, consistent citation of source references, and strong use of both absolute figures and derived margins. The narrative flows logically from summary to per-company breakdowns without redundancy. Margin calculations are explicitly stated rather than left implicit. Otherwise, the report is transparent about what the numbers show versus what they imply.
